seoTitle: How to Use AI Visuals and Forecasting in Power BI (2026)
metaDescription: Add time-series forecasting, Key Influencers, and Smart Narrative visuals in Power BI to surface trends without writing any code.
intro: Turning historical data into forward-looking insight usually means writing statistics code, but Power BI's built-in AI tools do this without any scripting. This guide covers adding a forecast line to a chart, tuning its confidence interval, and using Key Influencers and Smart Narrative to explain what's driving your metrics.

steps.heading: Build a line chart with a date axis
steps.body: Create a standard line chart with a continuous date column on the X-axis and your performance metric on the Y-axis.

steps.heading: Add a forecast
steps.body: Open the Analytics pane, scroll to the Forecast option, and click Add to generate a predictive trend line for your data.

steps.heading: Tune the forecast parameters
steps.body: Set your forecast length, adjust the seasonality to match your business cycle, and set the confidence interval, commonly around 95%.

steps.heading: Add Key Influencers
steps.body: Drop the Key Influencers visual onto the canvas, place your metric in the Analyze field and potential drivers in the Explain By field to see what factors move the number.

steps.heading: Add a Smart Narrative
steps.body: Insert the Smart Narrative visual to generate a dynamic text summary that updates automatically as users interact with slicers.

faq.question: Do I need to write any code to use these AI visuals?
faq.answer: No, forecasting, Key Influencers, and Smart Narrative are all built-in visuals that work through the standard formatting panel without scripting.

faq.question: How accurate is the built-in forecast?
faq.answer: Accuracy depends on your seasonality settings and data quality, so it's worth experimenting with the confidence interval and outlier settings for your specific dataset.

faq.question: What kind of chart works with the Forecast option?
faq.answer: The native Forecast analytics feature is designed for line charts with a continuous, uniform date axis.

faq.question: Does the Smart Narrative update automatically?
faq.answer: Yes, it rewrites its text description in response to filter and slicer changes made by the viewer.
